questions.question_text: A sales agent posts a property listing on social media without including the brokerage name. This is:
questions.correct_answer: A blind ad, which is prohibited
distractors: Permitted if the agent's name appears
questions.explanation: All advertising must include the brokerage name as registered with the Division. Advertising without brokerage identification is considered a blind ad and is prohibited regardless of the platform.

questions.question_text: Under HB 419, when advertising a property online, the brokerage name must be:
questions.correct_answer: Within one click of the advertisement
distractors: Displayed on every page of the website
In the same font size as the listing
Included in the meta tags only
questions.explanation: HB 419 (effective May 2025) established the 'one click' rule requiring the brokerage name to be accessible within one click of a digital property advertisement.

questions.question_text: An agent uses a team name in their advertising. Under Utah rules, what else must appear?
questions.explanation: Team names may be used in advertising, but the brokerage name as registered with the Division must also appear. The brokerage identification requirement cannot be replaced by a team name.

questions.question_text: A sales agent posts on social media that they are a licensed real estate agent but does not advertise any specific property or service. Under HB 419, this post is:
distractors: A blind ad requiring brokerage name
Required to include the broker's name
questions.explanation: Under HB 419, social media posts mentioning agent status that do not list a specific property or service are not considered advertisements and do not trigger brokerage identification requirements.

questions.question_text: All real estate advertising in Utah must be under the direction of the:
questions.explanation: All advertising must be under the direction of the principal broker. The PB is responsible for ensuring all advertising by affiliated licensees complies with Division rules and is truthful.

questions.question_text: A listing agent receives an offer but does not present it because they believe the price is too low. This is:
distractors: Required for offers under 80% of list price
Permitted if the seller is unavailable
questions.explanation: Failing to present all offers to the seller is specifically listed as an improper practice in Utah. The agent must present all offers regardless of their personal opinion about the terms.

questions.question_text: An agent tries to convince a buyer to break their purchase contract so the buyer can make an offer on the agent's own listing. This is:
questions.correct_answer: Inducing a party to break a contract
distractors: Legal if the new property is better
questions.explanation: Inducing a party to break an existing contract to enter into a new one is specifically prohibited as an improper practice in Utah, regardless of the motivation.

questions.question_text: Under HB 419, the sale of a manufactured home in Utah now requires:
distractors: Only a manufactured home dealer permit
questions.explanation: HB 419 (May 2025) requires that manufactured and mobile home sales involve a licensed real estate agent, expanding licensing requirements to cover these property types.

questions.question_text: A buyer's agent tells a lender the buyer earns $120,000 when the buyer actually earns $80,000. This is:
distractors: Acceptable to help the buyer qualify
Only illegal if the loan closes
questions.explanation: Representing a buyer's qualification status other than their true financial position to a lender is specifically listed as an improper practice. This is a form of fraud regardless of whether the loan closes.

questions.question_text: An agent purchases a property through their own listing without disclosing they are the buyer. This is:
distractors: Legal if a different agent writes the offer
questions.explanation: Acting in a dual capacity such as agent and undisclosed principal without disclosure is an improper practice. Licensees must disclose any personal interest in a transaction.

questions.question_text: A licensee advises a client that they cannot show a property to a family with children because the neighborhood is not family-friendly. This violates:
questions.explanation: Steering clients based on familial status violates fair housing laws. Licensees must comply with both federal and state fair housing laws and provide equal service regardless of protected class.

questions.question_text: A licensee drafts a custom contract clause for a client instead of using an approved form addendum. This constitutes:
questions.explanation: Licensees must not engage in the unauthorized practice of law. Drafting custom contract provisions is UPL; licensees may only fill in blanks on approved forms and recommend attorney consultation.

questions.question_text: Which obligation applies to all Utah licensees regardless of their role in a transaction?
distractors: Represent both buyer and seller equally
Provide legal advice on contract terms
questions.explanation: All licensees must act with honesty and integrity in all dealings. They are not required to represent both parties, provide legal advice, or conduct property inspections.

questions.question_text: A licensee has not completed their required continuing education. Continuing to practice without CE compliance:
distractors: Is acceptable until the next renewal date
Only matters if a complaint is filed
Is permitted with a grace period extension
questions.explanation: Licensees must maintain competence through continuing education. Practicing without meeting CE requirements can result in disciplinary action and an invalid license renewal.

questions.question_text: A licensee accepts compensation from both the buyer and seller in a transaction without telling either party. This is:
distractors: Standard practice if both parties benefit
Legal if the amounts are equal
questions.explanation: Accepting compensation from more than one party without full disclosure to all parties is an improper practice. All compensation arrangements must be transparent.
